title: HPLC Analysis of tRNA-Derived Nucleosides
author: Xingxing Chen
license_note: PMC full-text XML — Bio-protocol — CC-BY confirmed in article XML
materials: Synthetic complete yeast growth medium (Formedium, catalog number: CSC
1 M Tris-HCl, pH 7.4 (Santa Cruz, catalog number: SC-
1 M Tris-HCl, pH 8.3 (Fisher Scientific, catalog number: T
Qubit RNA Broad Range kit (Thermo Fisher, catalog number: Q10210 )
Bacterial alkaline phosphatase (BAP) (Sigma-Aldrich, catalog number: P

steps.instruction: 0.9% NaCl Store at RT. Reagent Final concentration Amount NaCl 0.9% (w/v) 9 g Nuclease-free H 2 O n/a 1,000 mL

steps.title: 10 mM ZnCl 2 Store at -20 °C
steps.instruction: 10 mM ZnCl 2 Store at -20 °C. Stable for at least five years. Reagent Final concentration Amount 0.1 M ZnCl 2 0.01 M 100 μL Nuclease-free H 2 O n/a 900 μL

steps.title: DE52 binding buffer Store at RT
steps.instruction: DE52 binding buffer Store at RT. Stable for at least one year. Reagent Final concentration Amount 1 M Tris-HCl, pH 7.4 0.1 M 100 mL NaCl 0.1 M 5.844 g Nuclease-free H 2 O n/a 900 mL

steps.title: DE52 solution Store at 4 °C
steps.instruction: DE52 solution Store at 4 °C. Stable for at least six months. Reagent Final concentration Amount DE52 0.33 g/mL (w/v) 100 g DE52 binding buffer n/a 300 mL

steps.title: tRNA elution buffer Store at RT
steps.instruction: tRNA elution buffer Store at RT. Stable for at least one year. Reagent Final concentration Amount 1 M Tris-HCl, pH 7.4 0.1 M 100 mL NaCl 1 M 58.44 g Nuclease-free H 2 O n/a 900 mL

steps.title: Nuclease P1 solution Store at -20 °C
steps.instruction: Nuclease P1 solution Store at -20 °C. Stable for at least one year. Reagent Final concentration Amount Nuclease P1 >250 units /1 mL 1 vial (>250 units) 1 M sodium acetate, pH 5.0 30 mM 30 μL Nuclease-free H 2 O n/a 970 μL

steps.title: HPLC solvent A Prepare before use
steps.instruction: HPLC solvent A Prepare before use. Adjust pH to 5.3 with NH 4 OH and H 3 PO 4 . Reagent Final concentration Amount 1 M NH 4 H 2 PO 4 0.01 M 10 mL Methanol 2.5% (v/v) 25 mL Milli-Q H 2 O n/a 965 mL

steps.title: HPLC solvent B Prepare before use
steps.instruction: HPLC solvent B Prepare before use. Adjust pH to 5.1 with NH 4 OH and H 3 PO 4 . Reagent Final concentration Amount 1 M NH 4 H 2 PO 4 0.01 M 10 mL Methanol 20% (v/v) 200 mL Milli-Q H 2 O n/a 790 mL

steps.title: HPLC solvent C Prepare before use
steps.instruction: HPLC solvent C Prepare before use. Reagent Final concentration Amount 1 M NH 4 H 2 PO 4 0.01 M 10 mL Acetonitrile 35% (v/v) 350 mL Milli-Q H 2 O n/a 640 mL Laboratory supplies

steps.title: Harvest 100 units (100 mL of...
steps.instruction: Harvest 100 units (100 mL of yeast culture at OD 600 =

steps.title: of yeast cells grown in synthetic...
steps.instruction: of yeast cells grown in synthetic complete yeast growth medium at log phase in 50 mL centrifuge tubes. Centrifuge the tubes at 1,000× g for 3 min at room temperature (RT; 25 °C) and remove the supernatant.

steps.title: Wash the cells once with 3 mL of 0
steps.instruction: Wash the cells once with 3 mL of 0.9% NaCl. Centrifuge at 1,000× g at RT for 3 min and remove the supernatant.

steps.title: Resuspend the cells in 8 mL...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the cells in 8 mL of phenol and vortex at 1,200 rpm using a benchtop mixer for 30 min at RT.

steps.title: Add 400 μL of chloroform and...
steps.instruction: Add 400 μL of chloroform and vortex at 1,200 rpm for an additional 15 min at RT. Centrifuge at 12,000× g for 20 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Collect the water phase in a...
steps.instruction: Collect the water phase in a 50 mL conical tube, add 4 mL of water-saturated phenol, and vortex at 1,200 rpm for 15 min at RT.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 12,000× g for 20 min at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 12,000× g for 20 min at 4 °C. Collect the water phase and mix it with 2.5 volumes of ethanol by inverting the tubes three times.

steps.title: Precipitate at -80 °C for 3...
steps.instruction: Precipitate at -80 °C for 3 h or -20 °C overnight.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 12,000× g for 20 min at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 12,000× g for 20 min at 4 °C. Remove the supernatant and dissolve the RNA pellet in 5 mL of DE52 binding buffer.

steps.title: Prepare a DE52 cellulose column by...
steps.instruction: Prepare a DE52 cellulose column by adding 5 mL of thoroughly mixed DE52 solution to a polypropylene column. Let the liquid pass through the column for approximately 5 min by gravity at RT.

steps.title: Add the resuspended RNA pellet to...
steps.instruction: Add the resuspended RNA pellet to the DE52 cellulose column and let the liquid pass through the column by gravity at RT.

steps.title: Add 7 mL of DE52 binding...
steps.instruction: Add 7 mL of DE52 binding buffer to the column and wait 5 min. Repeat the step once more.

steps.title: Elute the RNA by adding 7...
steps.instruction: Elute the RNA by adding 7 mL of tRNA elution buffer and collect the elute in a 15 mL centrifuge tube. Elute the tRNA once more by adding the elute back to the column to increase the yield.

steps.title: Add 5 mL of isopropanol to...
steps.instruction: Add 5 mL of isopropanol to the elute and mix by inverting the tube three times. Precipitate tRNA at -20 °C for at least 3 h.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 12,000× g for 20...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 12,000× g for 20 min at 4 °C to pellet tRNA. Discard the supernatant and resuspend the pellet in 1 mL of 70% EtOH.

steps.title: Transfer the resuspension to a 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the resuspension to a 1.5 mL centrifuge tube and spin at 16,000× g for 5 min at 4 °C to pellet tRNA.

steps.title: Dissolve the tRNA in 50 μL of nuclease-free water
steps.instruction: Dissolve the tRNA in 50 μL of nuclease-free water. Measure the tRNA concentration with Qubit RNA Broad Range kit. Approximately 200 μg of tRNA can be obtained from 100 units of yeast cells.

steps.title: The tRNA can be stored at...
steps.instruction: The tRNA can be stored at -80 °C for up to one week or continued with tRNA digestion. B. tRNA digestion

steps.title: Perform nuclease P1 digestion by preparing...
steps.instruction: Perform nuclease P1 digestion by preparing the following mix and incubating at 37 °C for 16 h. 50 μg tRNA x μL 10 mM ZnCl 2 5 μL Nuclease P1 solution 10 μL Nuclease-free H 2 O Add to 50 μL

steps.title: Dephosphorylate the P1-digested tRNA by preparing...
steps.instruction: Dephosphorylate the P1-digested tRNA by preparing the following mix and incubating at 37 °C for 2 h. Samples are ready for HPLC analysis. Digested tRNA 50 μL 0.5 M Tris-HCl pH 8.3 mM 20 μL BAP 4 μL C. HPLC analysis

steps.title: Prepare 1 L of each HPLC...
steps.instruction: Prepare 1 L of each HPLC solvent (A, B, and C; see Recipes).

steps.title: Put the solvent tubes (marked A,...
steps.instruction: Put the solvent tubes (marked A, B, or C) into the corresponding HPLC solvent. Solvent tube D will not be used and can be kept in water.

steps.title: Turn on both the HPLC separation...
steps.instruction: Turn on both the HPLC separation module and the PDA detector.

steps.title: Wet prime the HPLC system with...
steps.instruction: Wet prime the HPLC system with a flow rate of 4 mL/min for 4 min.

steps.title: Turn on the degas function
steps.instruction: Turn on the degas function.

steps.title: Open Empower 3 software
steps.instruction: Open Empower 3 software. Set up the gradient according to Table 1 . Table

steps.title: Gradient of the HPLC method
Set up the auto-injection volume to...
steps.instruction: Set up the auto-injection volume to 60 μL and the running time to 140 min for each sample.

steps.title: Transfer the digested tRNAs to the...
steps.instruction: Transfer the digested tRNAs to the 2 mL HPLC sample tube with the sample insert and cap.

steps.title: Update the sample inject table starting...
steps.instruction: Update the sample inject table starting with nuclease-free water as the first sample and DMSO as the second to clean the C30 column.

steps.title: Continue running the digested tRNA samples on the HPLC
steps.instruction: Continue running the digested tRNA samples on the HPLC.

description: Validation of protocol This protocol has been used and validated in the following research articles: • Xu et al. [8]. Sod1-deficient cells are impaired in formation of the modified nucleosides mcm 5 s 2 U and yW in tRNA. RNA ( Figures 1 –4, Supplementary Figure S1, and S2). • Xu et al. [9]. SSD1 suppresses phenotypes induced by the lack of Elongator-dependent tRNA modifications. PLOS Genetics (Supplementary Figure 5). • Xu et al. [10]. Identification of factors that promote biogenesis of tRNA CGA Ser . RNA Biology (Supplementary Figure S2). • Xu et al. [11]. Yeast Elongator protein Elp1p does not undergo proteolytic processing in exponentially growing cells. MicrobiologyOpen (Figure 4, panel B). • Chen et al. [12]. Elongator complex influences telomeric gene silencing and DNA damage response by its role in wobble uridine tRNA modification. PLOS Genetics (Figure 5). • Chen et al. [13]. Defects in tRNA modification associated with neurological and developmental dysfunctions in Caenorhabditis elegans elongator mutants. PLOS Genetics (Figure 2 and supplementary Figure S1–S3). • Huang et al. [14]. A genome-wide screen identifies genes required for formation of the wobble nucleoside 5-methoxycarbonylmethyl-2-thiouridine in Saccharomyces cerevisiae. RNA (Figure 2). • Björk et al. [15]. A conserved modified wobble nucleoside (mcm 5 s 2 U) in lysyl-tRNA is required for viability in yeast. RNA (Figure 5). • Esberg et al. [16]. Elevated levels of two tRNA species bypass the requirement for elongator complex in transcription and exocytosis. Molecular Cell (Supplementary Table 1 ). • Huang et al. [17]. An early step in wobble uridine tRNA modification requires the Elongator complex. RNA ( Table 1 and Figure 3). Figure 1. HPLC analysis of tRNA-derived nucleosides. A. Representative HPLC chromatography of modified nucleosides derived from yeast total tRNAs. B. Representative UV absorbance spectra of modified nucleosides. Abbreviations: absorbance unit (A.U.); 5-carbamoylmethylurid
